description: Blind 8 per-event guest data export. Filtered to attended guests only. Direct PII removed. Name-based fields resolved to guest IDs.
fieldsTransformed: guests.referredBy → referredByGuestId (name resolved to guest ID, null if unresolved)
feedback.spendTimeWith → spendTimeWithGuestIds (comma-separated names resolved to guest ID array)

guests.whyJoin: I want to join blind8 because I think it’s so cool to meet strangers and be able to connect and see what you relate to. I have tried time left when I was up in DC and had a good time and met quite a few people that I’m still friends with but now that I’m back in my hometown of Austin I haven’t gone back on time left but figured this is super cool too so why not give it a try to meet cool people from different backgrounds.
guests.socialBravery: My best example of social bravery is I also dine with strangers on the Timeleft app before but another form of social bravery I like is just going to farmers markets by myself or other events like I’ve gone to this collectible event and meeting and talking to all of these cool strangers and just becoming friends with them. One time, one of my current friends. I actually matched with her on hinge and without even meeting her yet asked her if she wanted to go to six flags with my friends and I the next day and she lived 2 hrs away from me. So I drove 2 hours picked her up and drove us to six flags and we had an amazing time and chilled at my place with all my friends then I drove her back that night and got home at like 4:30 am LOL
guests.passion: I could talk for hours about cooking or all things food. I love to cook and try new foods all the time. I could also talk for a long time about video games, kdramas, and traveling.
guests.screeningNotes: Born & raised in Austin, was at A&M, graduated spring of '24, went to Maryland for 1 year and a half for Masters in Finance, saw us on Tiktok a month ago, soft-spoken but can definitely tell a story, does things for the lore, will feel a sense of nervous excitement coming to Blind 8, wants to build friendships / relationships, loves to learn about people's lives and why people

guests.whyJoin: I just moved to Austin from Miami two weeks ago and like you guys am trying to make some lifelong connections and feel out the vibesss
guests.socialBravery: I know that social anxiety is scared of me, I go up to someone and start a conversation. I also work as a bartender so that helps filling in awkward gaps in conversation. My other job is mental health counseling where I see new clients which can take some bravery too.
guests.screeningNotes: She's a beast going to Japan for 2.5 weeks then coming back to a dinner in April

guests.whyJoin: I am looking to make friends. It’s been difficult to find my people in Austin with being in school full time for my PhD. I want to build community and connections in the city.
guests.socialBravery: Going to singles dating events by myself! I think it was very brave, as someone with anxiety. It’s hard to push myself out of my comfort zone at times.
guests.passion: Anything education excites me! I was a special ed teacher for 5 years and now I’m working on a PhD. I could go on for hours about disability rights!
guests.screeningNotes: From San Antonio, moved to Austin then Chicago. Currently in her first year of Phd - in learning disabilities and behavioral disorders. How teachers can provide the best care for students. Used to be a special ed teacher. Wants to be a professor one day. Went to UT for undergrad. Moved to Chicago for a few years then got into Phd. Making friends has been difficult for her. She's a little cut and dry. Like def not the hostel type. Will go out and do things by herself, but probably falls lower on social bravery scale. Her type of people are ambitious, outgoing, want to "network" she's a maybe - like would be fine at a dinner but not our archetype. Never has solo traveled or stayed in hostel. Has been to 13 to 14 diff countries.

guests.whyJoin: Recently moved to Austin from london and am still building my community with like minded individuals and explore the city beyond the lense of a tourist
guests.socialBravery: This past Valentine’s Day I got my single friends to write their numbers down on heart shaped post it notes and I had handed it out to the bout they fancied at latchkey
guests.passion: The intersection between design, fashion and architecture, how they navigate and evolve our identity
guests.screeningNotes:
Just moved from London, did undergrad in London, grad school in Austin, been here in for a month and a half, interior architecture, transferred from work to Houston (did not like Houston at all), does First Thursday, found us on Tiktok, (seen a couple of these community driven platforms - 222 or female driven (go with their friends that they've already established - get out of their comfort zone), wants to expand network (very confined to the friends she made in school), she's very nice, 

Handing out her friends' phone numbers, tried a hostel in Berlin (likes to pick a nice hotel because of lack of AC), feels safe to do Blind 8, 222 (has had dinner with strangers - feels comfortable with it), lot more intimate, a lot more generic

guests.whyJoin: Looking to build lasting friendships and create a home base in Austin centered around community and good times. Have been here for a year and still working on making solid friendships
guests.socialBravery: Since moving to Austin I’ve been very socially brave, I attend events alone most weekends, have done Timeleft and 222 events alone, I’ve traveled solo, I love meeting and connecting with strangers
guests.passion: Art, philosophy, pop culture, fitness, current events, interior design, vintage shopping, travel, thrifting
guests.screeningNotes: Didn’t know anyone moving to austin, from Sonoma (wine country), solo traveled to Eugene (Oregon), Boston, planning to go to Japan end of the year, really likes 222 over TimeLeft,

guests.occupation: Dancer, Slackliner, acro yogi, the arts
guests.whyJoin: I’m looking to expand my community and meet other cool people. I want to meet others that I could introduce to my community of dancers, slack liners, and acro yogies as well as be introduced to new communities. I love meeting people and it seems like I would be drawn to the types of people that are also drawn to this
guests.socialBravery: I went to an event full of strangers last week where you are put into small groups and then rotate. I meet some great people and I have already hung out with three of them separately outside of the event and two others are going to try out slacklining with me.
guests.passion: I love noticing how the relationships and friendships we’ve had with others have formed us. I love to go deep with people and understand how their experiences have shaped their viewpoints. On a lighter note… I love slacklining, dance, and acro yoga and enjoy introducing people to my hobbies. I also love to be a beginner and want to learn new things from others.

guests.whyJoin: I recently had to set some boundaries that caused me to lose a few friends. I’m looking for new, authentic connections in the city & am always down for a spontaneous adventure (hence the solo travel)
guests.socialBravery: My first solo trip I landed in Portugal without having booked a place to sleep that night. I never felt more alive & I fell in love with the unknown & novelty. I found that traveling solo, I was more approachable, leading to the most unique connections & experiences. Now I take at least one solo trip a year to reignite that energy.
guests.passion: Solo Travel, working out, mindfulness. The idea that two people can look at the same thing and have two completely different experiences
guests.screeningNotes: Been in Austin for 5 years. From Wisconsin. Loved the Hostel life. Thinks it's easy to meet people in Austin but hard to find people who are aligned with you. Studied abroad in the Netherlands. Last minute trip to Portugal on her own- feels she's never actually alone when solo traveling. Feels like she's a lot more approachable when she's not in a group. Went to a hostel happy hour and met a girl who was playing the violin in the hostel. Went on a walking tour in Portugal and saw a few people on the tour and struck up conversation with them and spent the rest of the night together - middle aged Italian woman and old London man who were giving her life advice.

guests.occupation: Entrepreneur in Real Estate, AI education and youth development.
guests.whyJoin: Love the idea of bringing strangers together. Huge see follower of Yes theory and Discover Connection, and this seems to follow suite. Plus, candidly, I am hoping to find someone not on a dating app 🤪
guests.socialBravery:
Backpacking with a guitar to 25+ countries and bringing people together at hostels for live karaoke sessions + hitting open mics whenever they come up. 

Favorite memory by far was hiking to Maccho Pichu in Peru and jamming out in a random tent in the middle of nowhere with a group of strangers also trying to survive the 50 mile trek
guests.screeningNotes: Used to own a bike shop. Seems like a super nice guy. graduated from school when he was 19. Big big traveler. Born and raised in Austin. Manages 4 houses - big into real estate. Super spontaneous. Got into Latin dance - now a latin dance instructor and in the music scene. 7
guests.notes: adventurer, loves to go to a city and travel 1 hr outside of the city and talk to locals, wants to hitchhike across the atlantic, hitchhiked from Croatia to Venice, built a 2 story house in Alaska, salsa instructor, drives a tesla, graduated college at 19yrs old

guests.occupation: Music, creative community events, poetry, dance, photography, eco-somatic facilitator, herbalist, Sufi whirling
guests.whyJoin: I love connecting with people! I host music and poetry community gatherings and there’s so much magic in bringing people into a space, especially when each person really wants to be there
guests.socialBravery: I host music and poetry events. I practice leading meditations that command a room to be listening in silence and presence. I share vulnerable songs and music
guests.passion: Sensuality, nature, art, music, herbalism. Poetry, communication, human design, dance. Travel stories, Sufi whirling
guests.screeningNotes: on tour with yoga influencer in europe & england, has a british voicemail, work with functional medicine, played the handpan, lived in Turkey (starting playing it with hippies in the street on Turkey), been in Austin (on & off) for 2 years, been back since December, started hosting music events (pebble sun creative expression sessions), ticket price for dinner & teas, curate the musicians to perform every time, a poet, published first full book,

guestConnections.note: Scott hosted Andre at his apartment
Went to a dance social he was djing
Met him at a dance social

eventGuests.hostNotes: {"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Loved be able to talk to her more one on one, super sweet, cool, want to hear more about architecture ","rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ_hangout":"true","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"We had fun talking about travel"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Super fun!! Love how spontaneous she is, always up for a dance battle. Needs to teach me to salsa","rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ_hangout":"true","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Kiara is just the best. Really brave she did her sidequest","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy_hangout":"true"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Super sweet, love hearing her why for moving out to Austin, I admire her boldness","rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ_hangout":"true","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Sweet easy to talk with. Can't wait to hear about her Japan adventures"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"So cool! Has such an amazing chill vibe, remind me of a happy little plant dancing in the wind. Can’t wait to hear more of her poetry and other art","rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ_hangout":"true","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Sonia was a comfort to talk with. Loved hearing how she could see my colors and how chill is to talk with her"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Super nice! Can tell he had more stories to tell! Would want to know if he would want to host and cook for blind8","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"My boy Anh-Ton is a great time. Loved seeing him letting lose."}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Super nice and sweet! Wish i talked to her more throughout the dinner, talked a bit in the fetti, can tell she’s fun to go out with and loved a side quest ","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Racheael was able to keep up with the group and found her way back to after party haha"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Has such a cool vibe, would love to hear more about her stories from Miami","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"My favorite half-cuban cant wait to actually go dancing with her","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy_hangout":"true"}

{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"So fun meeting someone from my high school, small world. Could tell she really had a fun time especially going out! ","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Bailee is awesome. Looking forward to hang out with her and Lennea and take her to Sammy Virjy for some untz untz"}
{"rGl6FRJurO8hn5sqcc6iQ":"Very helpful helping me with my tire, think he enjoyed my food! ","hoRkdgFUeffigjloOwlMy":"Andre absolute legend. Thank you for balancing out the ratios"}

feedback.improvement: I thought everything was fantastic! Possibly a bigger dinner table?
feedback.anythingElse: My schedule is packed until June, but would love for future opportunities to host!

feedback.highlight: The highlight of the night was hearing everyone’s stories of who they are and how they got there at dinner, and being able to shift seats to really be able to talk to the other half as well
feedback.improvement: I think it was an amazing experience especially with both of the hosts Diego and Kelsie they were both amazing and brought a bunch of energy and were amazing hosts. I don’t have any particular critiques at this time.

feedback.highlight: No other answer than a blind & pyramid
feedback.improvement: Carlo needs to not be a bad influence plz n ty my hangover the next day was his fault kidding!
feedback.anythingElse: What a fun experience, I am exponentially grateful to have been a part of this. Looking forward to toward the next milestones and memories

feedback.highlight: Conversations over dessert or cramming 8 into a Sudan after the bars
feedback.improvement: Some group games before going out
